shortAnswer: HbA1c shows the average blood sugar level over the past 2–3 months. Values above 6.5% may indicate diabetes. Values between 5.7% and 6.4% are considered prediabetes. Always discuss elevated values with your doctor.
metaDescription: HbA1c elevated? Normal ranges, diabetes risk, and what long-term blood sugar means — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Does my HbA1c indicate diabetes or prediabetes?
What dietary or lifestyle changes do you recommend?
pageTitle: HbA1c value — Long-term blood sugar explained | Medyra
